SOCIOECONOMIC: Home ownership status is recorded as a binary indicator of legal residential title.
Number of children and other dependents is derived from a detailed household roster.
Household size is determined by the count of all persons who reside together.
Length of employment with the current employer is measured in completed months.
Employment status is defined by the International Labour Organization classification criteria.
Occupation is classified according to the latest national occupational code standard.
Family income is the sum of gross annual earnings from all related household members.
Income is defined as the total pre-tax monetary receipts of an individual over one year.
Wealth is computed as net worth from all financial and non-financial assets minus debts.
Parents' income is the combined annual earnings of the respondent's mother and father.
Social class is determined by a composite index of occupation, education, and wealth.
Education level is recorded as the highest formal qualification or degree completed.
Total household gross income is aggregated from all income sources before taxes.
Disposable personal income is computed after the deduction of taxes and mandatory contributions.
Net financial assets are valued as the sum of bank accounts and investments minus non-mortgage debt.
Liquid net worth excludes illiquid assets like real estate and retirement accounts.
Investment income from dividends and interest is reported on the annual tax return.
Rental property income net of expenses is included in total household earnings.
Government transfer payments received are itemized by program type and amount.
Inheritance value received is recorded as a lump sum or asset transfer within the past five years.
Financial windfall from a lottery or legal settlement is recorded as a one-time event.
Total outstanding mortgage debt is balanced against the primary residence value.
Credit card revolving debt balance is taken from the most recent statement.
Student loan principal balance is obtained from the loan servicer's record.
Auto loan balance is reported for all vehicles financed by the household.
Wealth percentile rank is assigned by comparison to the national distribution.
Retirement savings account balance is reported for all defined contribution plans.
Emergency fund balance is defined as liquid assets sufficient for three months of expenses.
Taxable income is computed after all allowable deductions and exemptions are applied.
Alimony and child support payments received are added to the recipient's income total.
Financial support from family members outside the household is recorded as a separate transfer.
Number of income earners in the household is counted for the reference period.
Main source of household income is categorized as wages, self-employment, or transfers.
Annual wage and salary income from primary employment is reported by the worker.
Employment duration in the current job is measured in years and months of continuous service.
Total work experience since first entry into the labor force is recorded in years.
Unemployment spell duration in the past year is counted in weeks of active job search.
Underemployment status is defined as part-time work for economic reasons.
Temporary employment contract type is flagged for fixed-term or seasonal work.
Self-employment status is determined by ownership of a business with no paid employees.
Multiple job holder indicator is set to true if the person holds more than one job.
Industry sector is coded by the North American Industry Classification System.
Occupation title is transcribed from the employer's job description for the worker.
Managerial or supervisory responsibility indicator is derived from job duty questions.
Standard occupational classification major group is assigned at the two-digit level.
Job tenure with previous employer is captured for the last job held before current.
Reason for leaving last job is categorized as layoff, quit, or termination.
Full-time employment status is defined as usual weekly hours of thirty-five or more.
Hours worked per week in the main job is averaged over a typical month.
Night shift or rotating shift schedule indicator is reported by the respondent.
Work from home frequency is categorized as never, sometimes, or always.
Union membership status is verified by union card or dues payment record.
Professional license or certification requirement for the job is indicated by the employer.
Employment sector is classified as private, public, or non-profit.
Date of first employment after school completion is recorded for work history analysis.
Retirement status is determined by complete withdrawal from the labor force with pension.
Home ownership status is classified as owner with mortgage, owner free of mortgage, or renter.
Type of dwelling is recorded as single-family detached, apartment, or mobile home.
Number of bedrooms in the housing unit is counted from the building plan.
Year of home purchase is recorded for current owner-occupied residence.
Monthly mortgage payment is reported for all owner households with a mortgage.
Property tax annual amount is obtained from the local assessor's record.
Home equity value is computed as market price minus outstanding mortgage balance.
Renter's monthly rent payment is reported for the housing unit.
Subsidized housing indicator is flagged if the rent is government assisted.
Number of persons per room is calculated as household size divided by total rooms.
Household size composition includes counts of adults and children by age bracket.
Number of dependents under age six is counted for childcare need assessment.
Number of dependents aged sixty-five and older is recorded for elder care planning.
Presence of a grandparent in the household is indicated by a relationship code.
Multigenerational household flag is set if three or more generations co-reside.
Single-parent household indicator is derived from the presence of only one parent.
Child dependency ratio is computed as the number of children under eighteen per adult.
Total number of vehicles available to the household is reported from registration records.
Housing cost burden is defined as the ratio of housing costs to household income.
Overcrowded housing status is flagged when persons per room exceeds one point five.
Second home ownership status is recorded as a vacation or investment property indicator.
Length of residence at current address is measured in years since move-in date.
Highest educational credential is categorized as no diploma, high school, some college, bachelor's, or graduate.
Years of schooling completed is recorded as total years from first grade onward.
Field of study of the highest degree is coded by a detailed discipline classification.
Literacy and numeracy proficiency score is derived from a standardized assessment.
School enrollment status is reported for persons currently attending a formal program.
Parental education level is the highest degree held by the respondent's mother and father.
Parental occupation at the respondent's age fifteen is classified by prestige score.
Socioeconomic status index is constructed from education, income, and occupation variables.
Social class self-placement is assessed by a subjective rating on a ten-point scale.
Objective social class is assigned using the Goldthorpe class scheme for occupations.
Childhood poverty exposure indicator is flagged if family income was below the poverty line.
Cultural capital index is measured by participation in highbrow leisure activities.
Educational mobility is the difference between the respondent's and parents' years of schooling.
Occupational prestige score is assigned from the Standard International Occupational Prestige Scale.
Intergenerational income elasticity is estimated from parents' and own income ranks.
Wealth quintile at birth is assigned based on parents' asset holdings.
College selectivity level is categorized by the admission rate of the institution attended.
Vocational training certificate is recorded for skilled trades and technical programs.
Academic track in high school is categorized as general, vocational, or college preparatory.
Advanced Placement course count is reported from the high school transcript.
Degree field mismatch with current job is indicated by a dissimilarity index.
Social network diversity index is derived from the occupations of personal contacts.
